front: Short Story: the narrator and a blind man named Robert smoke weed, watch a TV documentary about the Middle Ages, and draw the title building together
Short Story: the blind guest lost his wife Beulah to cancer and tugs his beard as the TV plays
Short Story: two couples (a cardiologist and his wife with an abusive ex named Ed) drink gin and discuss the title concept; structured after Plato's Symposium
back: What We Talk About When We Talk About Love (Raymond Carver)
work: What We Talk About When We Talk About Love

front: Short Story: two characters argue over the words 'vassals' and 'vessels'; ends with them listening to 'the human noise we sat there making'
back: What We Talk About When We Talk About Love (Raymond Carver)
work: What We Talk About When We Talk About Love

front: Short Story: set at Frank Martin's rehab center; the narrator meets J.P., who tells about kissing his chimney sweep wife Roxy for good luck
Short Story: the narrator compares himself to the protagonist of Jack London's 'To Build a Fire' while debating calling his wife or girlfriend
Short Story: Claire is disturbed after her husband finds a dead girl on a camping trip and attends the girl's funeral
back: So Much Water So Close to Home (Raymond Carver)
work: So Much Water So Close to Home

front: Author: early works heavily edited by Gordon Lish, who told him to use 'five words in place of fifteen'; D.T. Max chronicled the controversy
Author: studied under John Gardner at Chico State College in 1958; called the 'American Chekhov'
Short Story: motel managers Holly and Duane discuss their failing marriage and Duane's affair with cleaning lady Juanita
